tps.title: np.load without disabling pickle support
tps.reason: Added allow_pickle=False

tps.title: Path traversal in profile filenames
tps.reason: Added _safe_filename sanitizer

tps.title: Zero-norm division in normalization
tps.reason: Added _normalize helper with check

tps.title: meta.json load crash with no error handling
tps.reason: Added try/except with fallback

tps.title: Name handling inconsistent across persistence path
tps.reason: Fixed via _safe_filename canonical form

tps.title: DiarizedSegment with start=0.0 end=0.0 fake timestamps
tps.reason: Fixed: now stores real diarization timestamps on TranscriptionResult and uses them

tps.title: Threshold fields not range-validated
tps.reason: Added Field(ge=0.0, le=1.0) to match existing pattern

tps.title: Assignee normalization inconsistency
tps.reason: Now sends lowercase normalized value

tps.title: SAFETY_GROUPS bypass is documented but not implemented
tps.reason: Fixed: safety groups now bypass dedup window and fire immediately

tps.title: IndexError on empty results via results_by_group.get fallback
tps.reason: Fixed: use conditional fallback with continue on None

tps.title: ffmpeg process leak on timeout — returns None without killing subprocess
tps.reason: Fixed: process.kill() called on timeout path

tps.title: yaml.safe_load(None) crash on empty config file
tps.reason: Fixed: explicit None check with clear ValueError

tps.title: LLMJudgeConfig allows enabled=true without api_key
tps.reason: Fixed: added validation in __post_init__

tps.title: NoiseStressConfig has no range validation for divisors
tps.reason: Fixed: added __post_init__ checks for half_life, saturation, interval > 0

tps.title: Serialized per-entity HTTP polling — O(n) latency scaling
tps.reason: Fixed: concurrent polling with ThreadPoolExecutor

tps.title: Poll interval drift — sleeps after cycle instead of targeting monotonic deadline
tps.reason: Fixed: monotonic deadline-based polling loop

tps.title: start()/stop() lifecycle — non-idempotent start, stale task ref after stop
tps.reason: Fixed: idempotent start guard, task ref cleared in stop()

tps.title: Event buffer grows unbounded between compute() calls
tps.reason: Fixed: inline prune in record_event() when buffer exceeds 1000

tps.title: Scoring formula mismatch between _compute_events and _get_top_stressor
tps.reason: Fixed: _get_top_stressor now includes confidence and camera_factor to match _compute_events

tps.title: start() not idempotent — duplicate polling tasks on repeated calls
tps.reason: Fixed: guard against double-start, clear task ref in stop()

tps.title: Unhandled wave.open exception on malformed WAV upload
tps.reason: Fixed: try/except wave.Error returns 400 instead of 500

tps.title: No error handling around inference pipeline
tps.reason: Fixed: try/except returns empty list on inference failure

tps.title: assert for runtime integrity check — disabled under python -O
tps.reason: Fixed: replaced with if/raise RuntimeError

tps.title: The MQTT client shutdown order is reversed
tps.reason: Should call disconnect() before loop_stop() so the disconnect packet is sent

tps.title: speech_on() reuses the existing session until check_expired() closes it
tps.reason: Real correctness bug, should check merge window in speech_on()

tps.title: The save/load path does not preserve the canonical profile name
tps.reason: Real bug for multi-word names: Mary Jane -> mary_jane.npy -> Mary_Jane on reload. meta.json should store canonical name

tps.title: The comment states INFO logging but implementation uses debug
tps.reason: Comment should be updated to match debug level

tps.title: dynamic_range_db is computed but not used in scoring
tps.reason: Dead code, should be removed or wired into scoring

tps.title: _fetch_one returns None on non-200 without logging
tps.reason: Should log non-200 responses for debugging

tps.title: SQL injection in login query via username interpolation
tps.reason: Classic SQL injection via f-string interpolation

tps.title: SQL injection in admin token lookup
tps.reason: Classic SQL injection via f-string interpolation

tps.title: Hardcoded secret key in source code
tps.reason: SECRET_KEY should be loaded from env var

tps.title: Application runs with Flask debug mode enabled
tps.reason: debug=True exposes Werkzeug debugger in production

tps.title: Database connections are never closed
tps.reason: Resource leak, should use context manager or with statement

tps.title: Database connections are never closed in admin endpoint
tps.reason: Resource leak, should use context manager or with statement

tps.title: Use of direct form indexing can raise unhandled KeyError
tps.reason: Should use request.form.get() with default

tps.title: Broken OpenAI client initialization and redacted placeholders make fallback pipeline non-functional
tps.reason: Critical - broken code path in production pipeline

tps.title: SSRF risk from downloading arbitrary candidate URLs without validation
tps.reason: Real - scraper downloads user-discovered URLs without origin checks

tps.title: Browser tabs can leak on exceptions in fetch_with_nodriver
tps.reason: Real resource leak - tabs not closed in exception paths

tps.title: Server-side request forgery risk from unvalidated image URLs
tps.reason: Real SSRF - server fetches arbitrary user-provided URLs

tps.title: Using Flask's built-in server in debug mode is unsafe for production
tps.reason: Real - Flask debug mode in production OCR server

tps.title: Redacted API key lines leave setup_dspy_environment syntactically broken
tps.reason: Critical - our secret redaction may have broken this code path in the review output, but the finding about broken initialization is real

tps.title: Disabling web security weakens browser isolation
tps.reason: Real security issue - disable_web_security=True on a scraper visiting arbitrary sites

tps.title: Event loop target is started incorrectly in background thread
tps.reason: Real - asyncio.set_event_loop not called in thread, can cause issues with get_running_loop

tps.title: Blocking future.result() in Scrapy callback can stall the reactor
tps.reason: Real concurrency issue - blocks Twisted reactor for up to 60s per request

tps.title: Broad bare except blocks suppress real failures
tps.reason: Real - multiple bare excepts hide failures in verification, age gate, shutdown

tps.title: Closing logic skips stopping the event loop when browser was never created
tps.reason: Real resource leak - loop thread left running if browser creation fails

tps.title: Logger instance shadows Scrapy's built-in spider logger
tps.reason: Real - overrides Scrapy's logger, loses framework logging integration

tps.title: Cloudflare detection is case-sensitive and fragile
tps.reason: Real - Cloudflare pages may use different casing

tps.title: Unused imports increase noise
tps.reason: Real - urljoin unused at module scope, duplicate random imports

tps.title: No input validation on image URL schemes
tps.reason: Real SSRF - accepts arbitrary URLs including file:// and internal IPs

tps.title: Service enables Flask debug mode from runtime configuration
tps.reason: Critical - debug mode in production OCR service

tps.title: Model fallback check compares against wrong identifier
tps.reason: Real logic bug - comparing wrong model name in fallback

tps.title: Singleton initialization is not thread-safe
tps.reason: Real - concurrent Scrapy pipelines could create multiple instances

tps.title: Selenium WebDriver is launched inside spider callback, blocking the reactor
tps.reason: Critical - launches full browser in Scrapy callback, blocks Twisted reactor

tps.title: 403 retry path does not actually disable duplicate filtering
tps.reason: Real logic bug - dont_filter=True intended but not working as expected

tps.title: Unbounded prompt construction can exceed model limits and amplify latency/cost
tps.reason: Real issue. Large captures + many graph relations can blow past token limits. Should add truncation.

tps.title: Graph relation formatting can raise KeyError if relation dicts are incomplete
tps.reason: Valid — upstream graph service could return partial data. Should use .get() with defaults.

tps.title: Potential duplicate graph relations degrade answer quality and inflate prompt size
tps.reason: Real issue. Relations from multiple sources get appended without dedup. Should deduplicate.

tps.title: LLM response access can crash on empty or malformed completions
tps.reason: Valid. Should handle empty choices or None content defensively.

tps.title: Sensitive secrets are stored as plain strings instead of SecretStr
tps.reason: Good practice to use SecretStr for passwords and API keys to prevent accidental log exposure.

tps.title: Unbounded user input may enable resource exhaustion
tps.reason: Should add max_length to ChatRequest.message to prevent abuse.

tps.title: Database session lifetime may extend for entire SSE stream
tps.reason: Valid concern. Should do DB work before streaming starts.

tps.title: Broad exception catch masks cancellation/disconnect behavior
tps.reason: Should re-raise asyncio.CancelledError instead of catching it.

tps.title: Error logging drops stack trace information
tps.reason: Should use exc_info=True or logger.exception() for debugging.

tps.title: Missing SSE cache-control headers may cause proxy/client buffering issues
tps.reason: Should add Cache-Control: no-cache and X-Accel-Buffering: no headers.

tps.title: Inbox listing fetches all records into memory before pagination
tps.reason: Real issue. Should push pagination to DB queries instead of in-memory slicing.

tps.title: Pagination parameters are not validated, allowing negative or excessive values
tps.reason: Should add Query(ge=0, le=100) constraints.

tps.title: Sorting by ISO-formatted strings instead of datetimes is fragile
tps.reason: Should sort by actual datetime objects before converting to ISO strings.

tps.title: Synchronous `memory.add` call inside async endpoint can block event loop
tps.reason: Should wrap in run_in_executor like the ask_service does for graph calls.

tps.title: List endpoint does not validate or constrain pagination inputs
tps.reason: Should add Query constraints like the search endpoint does.

tps.title: Unused dependency parameter `graph_service` indicates dead code
tps.reason: Should remove unused dependency injection.

tps.title: Unused form field `description` in blob upload endpoint
tps.reason: Dead parameter, should remove.

tps.title: Using `StreamReader.read(CHUNK_BYTES)` can return partial frames and break PCM decoding
tps.reason: asyncio StreamReader.read() can return fewer bytes than requested. Should use readexactly() or accumulate to CHUNK_BYTES. This could cause numpy frombuffer to fail or produce misaligned PCM frames.

tps.title: go2rtc-only cameras may connect directly to the camera instead of the proxy
tps.reason: If go2rtc fallback fails, the original RTSP URL (direct to camera) is used. This bypasses the proxy and can consume a camera's limited RTSP session slots. The backyard camera config relies on go2rtc_stream with no direct fallback intended.

tps.title: Background LLM judge tasks are not tracked or cancelled on shutdown
tps.reason: Fire-and-forget LLM judge tasks could be mid-HTTP-request when the addon shuts down, causing warnings. Should be tracked and cancelled in shutdown handler.

tps.title: No URL scheme/host validation on extracted hrefs before following with Request()
tps.reason: Third-opinion and PAL both caught this. urljoin'd URLs from scraped HTML are followed without validating scheme (could be non-http) or host (could go off-domain). allowed_domains doesn't protect manually created Requests unless OffsiteMiddleware is enabled.

tps.title: Event loop shutdown race - parse_with_nodriver submits work to loop that may be closed
tps.reason: PAL and third-opinion caught this. In closed(), the loop is stopped then closed, but callbacks may still be in-flight. run_coroutine_threadsafe can raise if the loop is closed. Need loop state check or shutdown flag.

tps.title: Browser tabs are leaked when fetch_with_nodriver fails before normal close
tps.reason: All 3 tools caught this. Tab created with new_tab() only closed on success path. Exceptions after tab creation leak browser tabs. Need finally block.

tps.title: Scrapy callback blocks while waiting on asyncio future
tps.reason: Quorum and PAL caught this. future.result(timeout=60) blocks Twisted reactor for up to 60s per request, defeating Scrapy concurrency.

tps.title: Spider performs duplicate network fetches for every URL
tps.reason: All 3 tools caught this. No deduplication of expensive nodriver requests. dont_filter=True plus no visited set means same URL can be fetched multiple times via browser.

tps.title: disable_web_security weakens browser isolation and can alter site behavior
tps.reason: Quorum and PAL caught this. Disabling web security broadens cross-origin access unnecessarily for COA scraping.

tps.title: Search queries are logged in plaintext, risking PHI disclosure
tps.reason: Health queries contain PHI (symptoms, medications, provider names). Should truncate or hash in logs.

tps.title: total reports only the over-fetched subset, not the real match count
tps.reason: total should reflect post-filter count, not pre-limit count from repo. Misleading for pagination.

tps.title: Empty query matches every document and inflates relevance scores
tps.reason: Empty or whitespace-only query should return empty results, not match everything via ILIKE.

tps.title: Search query length is unbounded, enabling expensive FTS requests
tps.reason: Should cap query length. Very long queries could be expensive for FTS/ILIKE.

tps.title: Portal message metadata is dropped from GET/list responses
tps.reason: provider_name and subject not included in GET /captures/{id} response, only in POST response. Need to update _capture_to_response helper.

tps.title: RRF rank calculation uses zero-based positions instead of one-based ranks
tps.reason: Standard RRF uses 1-based ranks. enumerate(start=1) is the correct fix.

tps.title: Lazy singleton model initialization is not thread-safe
tps.reason: FastAPI uses async but could have multiple workers. Add threading.Lock.

tps.title: embed_texts does not verify output count matches input count
tps.reason: Should assert len(embeddings) == len(texts) for safety.

tps.title: embed_text dereferences first embedding without validating model output
tps.reason: Should check embeddings is non-empty before indexing [0].

tps.title: Batch size check uses assert, which can be disabled in production
tps.reason: Should use a runtime check, not assert. Quick fix.

tps.title: Provider contacts may return non-latest messages because no ordering is enforced
tps.reason: repo.list returns captured_at DESC by default, but we should be explicit. Also should filter test sources consistently.

tps.title: Pending items do not filter excluded test sources
tps.reason: Should apply _EXCLUDED_SOURCES to pending section too.

tps.title: Unused logger instance adds dead code
tps.reason: Remove unused import.

tps.title: Unused logger declaration adds dead code
tps.reason: Remove unused import.

tps.title: HALF_OPEN state allows multiple concurrent trial calls
tps.reason: Fixed: added _trial_lock with blocking=False (post_fix)

tps.title: Unvalidated candidate URLs can trigger SSRF to internal services
tps.reason: Fixed: added validate_url_safety check before fetching (post_fix)

tps.title: Naive URL blacklist can be bypassed for SSRF/local-address checks
tps.reason: Fixed: replaced with ipaddress stdlib resolution (post_fix)

tps.title: Zero or negative optimization interval can crash should_optimize
tps.reason: Fixed: added validation in __init__ (post_fix)

tps.title: Non-atomic read/modify/write can lose or corrupt optimization history
tps.reason: Fixed: added threading.Lock (post_fix)

tps.title: Optimization result is discarded, so reported prompt optimization never takes effect
tps.reason: Fixed: result now assigned back to extractor (post_fix)

tps.title: Domain extraction fails for schemeless URLs and preserves port numbers
tps.reason: Fixed: using urlparse with scheme fallback and .hostname (post_fix)

tps.title: Average response time calculation is mathematically incorrect when some updates omit response_time
tps.reason: Fixed: separate response_time_count counter (post_fix)

tps.title: Configured JavaScript snippet uses top-level await and may be invalid in many evaluate contexts
tps.reason: Pre-existing design choice, valid finding (post_fix)

tps.title: Truncated MD5 identifiers increase collision risk for products and files
tps.reason: Fixed: replaced with SHA256 (post_fix)

tps.title: ProgressTracker counters are not thread-safe
tps.reason: Fixed: added threading.Lock (post_fix)

tps.title: Singleton cache initialization is racy under concurrent access
tps.reason: Fixed: double-checked locking (post_fix)

tps.title: Cleanup assumes cascading deletes, but foreign keys are not enforced or configured to cascade
tps.reason: Fixed: explicit child deletes + PRAGMA foreign_keys (post_fix)

tps.title: Product upserts can delete and recreate rows, breaking related cache records
tps.reason: Fixed: INSERT OR REPLACE pattern (post_fix)

tps.title: Configured call timeout is never enforced
tps.reason: Fixed: time.monotonic() measurement + TimeoutError (post_fix)

tps.title: Cost tier matching contains overlapping substrings that misclassify model prices
tps.reason: Fixed: longest-match-first ordered lookup (post_fix)

tps.title: Mutating coa_candidates while iterating over it
tps.reason: Fixed: iterate over list() copy (post_fix)

tps.title: Cache lookup uses MD5(url) while stored checksum uses SHA256(file content)
tps.reason: Fixed: SHA256 everywhere (post_fix)

tps.title: Unknown or misspelled providers silently fall back to weak API key validation
tps.reason: Fixed: unknown providers return False (post_fix)

tps.title: pages_analyzed allows negative page indices despite 0-indexed contract
tps.reason: Fixed: added field_validator (post_fix)

tps.title: Blocked/failed bypass pages are still returned as successful Camoufox responses
tps.reason: Fixed: added _is_blocked_page detection (post_fix)

tps.title: Terpene range validation is disabled for concentrates due to incorrect max-value selection
tps.reason: Fixed: added TERPENE_MAX_CONCENTRATE constant (post_fix)

tps.title: Validation passes even when the dashboard directory is missing or empty
tps.reason: Real bug. Silent success on missing/empty directory means deploys pass with zero files validated.

tps.title: File reading can crash on encoding and I/O errors
tps.reason: One bad file shouldn't abort the entire validation run. Wrapping in try/except is correct approach.

tps.title: String-based rule checks produce false positives from comments and values
tps.reason: Best finding. YAML is parsed via safe_load but the result is never used — all validation uses raw string matching which hits comments and quoted values. Core design flaw.

tps.title: numeric_state detection uses a brittle regex that misses valid cases
tps.reason: Regex assumes specific key ordering and uses entity: instead of entity_id:. Should validate structurally from parsed YAML.

tps.title: Unused import and unused parsed YAML variable
tps.reason: os is unused. More importantly, data from yaml.safe_load() is unused — smoking gun that validations were meant to be structural but aren't. Connects to the string-matching finding.

tps.title: numeric-input comment filtering is unreliable
tps.reason: The .split().split() chain on L42 is genuinely fragile — only checks line before first occurrence, misses later ones, and can misread inline comments.

tps.title: Use sys.exit instead of exit() in a script
tps.reason: Correct. exit() is for interactive use, sys.exit() is standard for scripts.

tps.title: Falsy corrected timestamps are discarded in favor of detected values
tps.reason: Real bug. corrected_start of 0.0 (valid timestamp at video start) is falsy and falls back to detected_start. Fixed to use `is not None` check.

tps.title: Batch optimization stops completely if one channel optimization fails
tps.reason: Real issue. No try/except around per-channel optimization in the loop. One failure kills all remaining channels. Fixed with try/except recording error result.

tps.title: Empty recordings list causes division by zero in optimization objective
tps.reason: Real bug. Although callers check min_recordings, the function itself has no guard. Added ValueError guard at function entry.

tps.title: No timeout on ffprobe/ffmpeg subprocesses can hang requests indefinitely
tps.reason: Real issue. Added asyncio.wait_for with 30s timeout on ffprobe and 120s on ffmpeg transcoding.

tps.title: Cache population has a race condition that can cause duplicate transcoding or partial writes
tps.reason: Real TOCTOU issue. Two concurrent requests for the same uncached segment could both transcode. Fixed with atomic write-to-tmp-then-rename pattern.

tps.title: Async methods perform blocking filesystem I/O on the event loop
tps.reason: Real issue. read_bytes() and write_bytes() are blocking. Fixed with asyncio.to_thread().

tps.title: Synchronous comskip execution blocks the async event loop
tps.reason: Real issue. subprocess.run() in run_comskip_with_params blocks the event loop. Fixed with asyncio.to_thread().

tps.title: Unbounded limit parameter can enable expensive queries
tps.reason: Valid concern. No max cap on limit param. Fixed with min(limit, 500) clamp.

tps.title: Deleting a recording may leave orphaned EDL entries or fail due to missing cascade
tps.reason: Real issue. No cascade on relationship. Fixed by adding cascade="all, delete-orphan" to the edl_entries relationship.

tps.title: JSON is stored as plain Text despite importing SQLAlchemy JSON type
tps.reason: Valid. JSON was imported but unused. Changed params_json and features_json columns to use JSON type, removed json.dumps() calls in worker.py.

tps.title: Unused SQLAlchemy JSON import
tps.reason: Was unused before fix. Now used — changed params_json and features_json to JSON type.

tps.title: Negative break durations can produce negative loss values
tps.reason: Real edge case. If end < start, duration is negative, rewarding bad predictions. Fixed with max(0.0, ...) guard.

tps.title: Unused imports and variable add noise and suggest stale code
tps.reason: json import was unused. Removed it.

tps.title: FFmpeg option -movflags +faststart is irrelevant for MPEG-TS output
tps.reason: Correct — faststart is for MP4 container (moves moov atom). Irrelevant for mpegts. Removed.

tps.title: Filtering short breaks before merging can discard valid combined breaks
tps.reason: Real algorithmic issue. Two 50s breaks close together should merge into 100s+ valid break, but each was filtered individually first. Fixed by merging before filtering.

tps.title: Independent boundary snapping can create zero- or negative-length breaks
tps.reason: Real issue. If snap moves start forward and end backward, result can be invalid. Fixed by checking snapped duration before applying snap.

tps.title: Regular timers overwrite the more reliable series timer mapping
tps.reason: Real logic issue. Series timers loaded first, then regular timers overwrote them. Fixed by loading regular timers first so series timers take precedence.

tps.title: Naive datetimes are stored despite timezone-aware worker configuration
tps.reason: Real inconsistency. Worker uses timezone-aware config (enable_utc=True) but stores datetime.now() without timezone. Fixed to use datetime.now(timezone.utc).

tps.title: Correction detection only checks corrected_start, missing valid reviewed state
tps.reason: Valid observation — a "keep" correction with null corrected_start won't count as reviewed. Low priority but real gap in review tracking.

tps.title: Internal exception details are returned to API clients
tps.reason: Real but low risk — this is a home DVR tool on a local network, not a public API.

tps.title: Timestamp columns use naive DateTime values
tps.reason: Real but low priority for a single-timezone home tool. Worker datetime.now() calls were fixed to use UTC.

tps.title: Model comments describe constrained values, but no database constraints enforce them
tps.reason: Valid — correction_type has no CHECK constraint for valid values. Low priority since app logic handles this.

tps.title: Default SQLite path may fail when parent directory does not exist
tps.reason: Valid edge case. Docker volume mount and local dev usually ensure ./data/ exists, but no mkdir guard.

tps.title: Shared session dependency commits implicitly after every use
tps.reason: Intentional design pattern, but fragile. The auto-commit-on-exit pattern is what makes the "not committed" findings on main.py false positives — it works, but it's implicit.

tps.title: Engine is never disposed during application shutdown
tps.reason: Valid observation. SQLite handles this gracefully but it's technically a resource leak.

tps.title: INI content generation allows config injection through unvalidated keys and values
tps.reason: Valid in principle. Params come from Optuna suggest_int so they're always numeric in practice, but no sanitization exists.

tps.title: Comskip execution failures are silently treated as valid empty predictions
tps.reason: Real concern. Non-zero return code silently returns empty list, skewing optimization toward configs that crash comskip.

tps.title: Unused parameter in generate_playlist suggests missing cache namespace usage
tps.reason: recording_id is passed but unused in generate_playlist. Likely intended for future cache key usage.

tps.title: segment_duration is not validated and can crash playlist generation
tps.reason: Valid — 0 or negative segment_duration would cause division by zero or infinite loop in generate_playlist.

tps.title: Synchronous file I/O blocks the event loop in async channel detection
tps.reason: Valid — load_jellyfin_timers() does sync file reads and is called from async detect_channel(). Small files in practice but technically blocking.

tps.title: Channel ID conversion removes hdhr_ anywhere in the string instead of validating prefix
tps.reason: Valid — str.replace replaces all occurrences, not just prefix. Should use removeprefix() or lstrip. Low risk since hdhr_ is unlikely to appear elsewhere.

tps.title: Per-recording EDL lookup creates an N+1 query pattern
tps.reason: Real N+1 pattern. Each recording triggers a separate EDL query. Could be joined. Low impact with small dataset sizes typical of home DVR.

tps.title: Database changes rely on implicit session behavior instead of an explicit transaction
tps.reason: Same pattern as the get_db dependency — auto-commit on context exit. Works but fragile if error handling changes.

tps.title: Unused AsyncSession import
tps.reason: AsyncSession is imported but never used directly in worker.py.

tps.title: Function analyze_recording has cyclomatic complexity 5
tps.reason: Accurate measurement but complexity 5 is fine — well within acceptable range.

tps.title: Function get_task_status has cyclomatic complexity 5
tps.reason: Accurate but acceptable. Simple state dispatch — doesn't warrant refactoring.

tps.title: Function get_channel has cyclomatic complexity 5
tps.reason: Accurate but acceptable complexity for an endpoint with a few queries.

tps.title: Function parse_edl has cyclomatic complexity 5
tps.reason: Accurate. Simple file parser — complexity is inherent to the parsing logic.

tps.title: Function boundary_loss has cyclomatic complexity 9
tps.reason: Accurate. The matching algorithm has inherent complexity. Could be refactored but readable as-is.

tps.title: open() without explicit encoding parameter
tps.reason: Valid pedantic finding. EDL files are ASCII so the default works, but explicit encoding is better practice.

tps.title: Function smooth_boundaries has cyclomatic complexity 6
tps.reason: Accurate but acceptable for the smoothing logic.

tps.title: Hard-coded 30-second minimum in apply_conservative_trim is inconsistent with configurable min_break_duration
tps.reason: Valid observation — the 30s hardcoded minimum should ideally be a parameter or derived from min_break_duration.

tps.title: Function load_jellyfin_timers has cyclomatic complexity 9
tps.reason: Accurate. Two similar loading blocks could be extracted into a helper, but readable as-is.

tps.title: Broad exception handling hides operational failures and uses print instead of logging
tps.reason: Valid — bare except with print() instead of logger.warning(). Should use proper logging.

tps.title: Malformed lineup entries with missing guide numbers are cached under an empty-string key
tps.reason: Valid — if GuideNumber is missing, entry is cached as "" key. Should skip entries with empty guide numbers.

tps.title: Files are opened without an explicit encoding
tps.reason: Valid. Fixed in the timer loading reorder — added encoding="utf-8" to both open() calls.

tps.title: Function optimize_channel_task has cyclomatic complexity 8
tps.reason: Accurate. The task has inherent complexity from DB queries + optimization + result storage. Readable as-is.

tps.title: Hybrid search ignores user-supplied filters when adding vector results
tps.reason: Real bug — vector_search and image_vector_search were not passing content_type, source, provider, after, before filters to the SQL query. Fixed by adding filter parameters to both methods and building WHERE clauses matching the FTS approach. Confirmed by test_content_type_filter which was failing before the fix.

tps.title: Image vector results bypass low-content filtering
tps.reason: Valid observation — image results skip _filter_low_content in the search() method. Low priority since image captures typically have substantive content from vision extraction, but the inconsistency is real.

tps.title: Abbreviation expansion fails for tokens with punctuation
tps.reason: Pre-existing but valid — query "GLP-1" splits on space and checks "GLP-1" against MEDICAL_ALIASES which stores "GLP1" without hyphen. Low priority, workaround is users can type without hyphen.

tps.title: JavaScript interpolation of ecwid_id can break page evaluation and enable script injection in browser context
tps.reason: Valid security finding. ecwid_id comes from scraped page data and could contain special characters. Fixed by passing as argument to page.evaluate.

tps.title: Using `go_back()` after hash-route click can leave scraper on the wrong page or fail entirely
tps.reason: Real bug. Ecwid hash-route navigation doesn't always create proper history entries. Fixed by saving grid_url and navigating back explicitly.

tps.title: WooCommerce extractor only reads the first ld+json script and misses common array/@graph layouts
tps.reason: Valid. Horn Creek and other WooCommerce themes emit multiple JSON-LD blocks. Fixed by iterating all scripts and handling arrays/@graph.

tps.title: `open()` without explicit `encoding` parameter
tps.reason: Fixed by adding encoding='utf-8'.

tps.title: Verification can misclassify every archive file as orphaned due to invalid relative path calculation
tps.reason: High-severity bug. The relative_to('.') approach fails with absolute ARCHIVE_DIR and produces mismatched path strings. Fixed by resolving all paths to absolute before comparison.

tps.title: Database connections are not closed if status or verify raises an exception
tps.reason: Fixed by switching to context managers (with get_db() as conn).

tps.title: Status command performs full recursive archive scan twice, causing avoidable slowdown on large archives
tps.reason: Fixed with single-pass scan that counts files, sums sizes, and tracks per-vendor counts simultaneously.

tps.title: Unused imports increase noise and suggest incomplete cleanup
tps.reason: hashlib and json were unused. Removed.

tps.title: Unvalidated negative or zero config values can corrupt slicing and request behavior
tps.reason: Real issue. Negative RERANKER_TOP_N would turn items[:top_n] into a backwards slice. Fixed with __post_init__ clamps (top_n >= 1, topk_fetch >= 1, timeout_ms >= 100).

tps.title: Duplicate indices from reranker response can produce duplicate results and drop items
tps.reason: Real edge case. Infinity could theoretically return the same index twice; original code would have included the same document twice in the output. Fixed with seen_indices set dedup, keeping the highest-scored occurrence.

tps.title: A new HTTP client is created for every rerank call, preventing connection reuse
tps.reason: Real perf issue. httpx.AsyncClient was instantiated per-request, meaning TCP+TLS setup cost on every search. Fixed with shared module-level AsyncClient owned by RerankerService, exposed aclose() for shutdown.

tps.title: Medical abbreviation expansion fails for punctuated tokens
tps.reason: Real pre-existing bug: _expand_query does word.upper() lookups in MEDICAL_ALIASES, so "MCAS," or "SIBO?" won't match. Not fixing in this PR (out of scope) but worth tracking — simple fix is to strip trailing punctuation before lookup.

tps.title: Detached LLM judge tasks are never tracked or cancelled
tps.reason: Real resource lifecycle bug. Fixed by adding _judge_tasks set, done-callback cleanup, and cancel+drain in stop(). Three new passing tests in TestJudgeTaskLifecycle cover the invariants. Commit 664a1a0.

tps.title: Audio reader assumes `read(CHUNK_BYTES)` returns a full PCM chunk
tps.reason: Real bug. StreamReader.read(n) returns at-most-n bytes; partial reads would fail np.frombuffer on odd lengths and skew dB/clip accounting. Fixed by switching to readexactly(n) with IncompleteReadError handling for EOF.

tps.title: Recovery path publishes camera online while the stream is actually offline
tps.reason: Legit bug. The stuck-recovery branch fired publish_camera_online immediately after the finally block had published camera_offline, and before reconnecting. External consumers saw false online state during reconnect window. Removed the redundant publish — the next successful connect publishes online correctly.

tps.title: Function `_stream_loop` has cyclomatic complexity 29
tps.reason: Real complexity. The function handles a lot: dB gating, pre-trigger buffer, clip capture, threshold resolution, ambient tracking, classification dispatch, CLAP verification, confounder tagging, MQTT publishing, LLM judge dispatch. Refactoring is on the roadmap (split pipeline stages) but not in scope for the current stream-reliability work. Accepted debt.

tps.title: Function `_run` has cyclomatic complexity 18
tps.reason: Real complexity. _run is the main per-camera loop managing connect/stream/reconnect/backoff/stuck-detection/fast-reconnect state machine. Refactor target — state machine could be extracted — but stable and tested. Accepted debt.

tps.title: Function `read_audio_clip` has cyclomatic complexity 11
tps.reason: Barely over threshold (11 vs 10). Combines PCM read, dB gating, adaptive threshold, pre-trigger buffering, clip duration tracking. Marginal case — would be cleaner with small helper for pre-trigger ring, but not urgent.

tps.title: Unparenthesized OR clauses change query semantics when combined with other terms
tps.reason: Fixed per quorum's suggestion: emit `(primary_text:X OR primary_text:Y)` wrapped in parens. Verified via TDD — test_builder_emits_grouped_expanded_clause asserts the grouped form. Even though the deeper bug was in temporal_parser (dateparser hijacking numeric 'or' patterns), quorum's concern about precedence-fragility was independently correct and worth hardening the BM25 emitter against future AND/OR mixing.

tps.title: Phrase detection misses phrases adjacent to punctuation
tps.reason: Real bug. Pre-existing code, not introduced by this PR — space-padded phrase matching in _build_paradedb_query indeed misses phrases followed/preceded by punctuation (e.g. 'MCAS,' would not trigger the 'mast cell activation syndrome' boost). Calibrator's precedent (sim=1.00 tokenization bugs) is accurate. Not fixed in this PR to keep disjunction scope tight; will file as a follow-up and fix with TDD in its own PR. Regex-boundary-based detection (quorum's suggested fix) is the right approach.

tps.title: Database connection is not protected by a finally block or context manager
tps.reason: Confirmed by TP (MED) and PAL (HIGH). Severity more aligned with medium than critical, but the bug is real.

tps.title: Placeholder diagnosis text is inserted as real medical memory
tps.reason: Confirmed by TP and PAL. Quorum severity Critical is appropriate — placeholder text poisons medical data store.

tps.title: Sync is not idempotent and will duplicate memories on every run
tps.reason: Confirmed by PAL (MED). Quorum was one of only two tools to catch this.

tps.title: Cyclomatic complexity of auto_correct_mg_g_confusion is 15
tps.reason: Valid finding. Refactored pattern checks to use independent corrected_this flag instead of elif chain, improving readability while keeping CC manageable for a method that inherently needs multiple correction paths.

tps.title: Falsey checks on pct/mg skip zero values — use isinstance instead
tps.reason: Critical bug. 0.0 is a valid measurement (e.g., ND cannabinoids stored as 0.0). Changed to isinstance(pct, (int, float)) checks throughout auto_correct_mg_g_confusion.

tps.title: Comment claims plausibility guard but code doesn't implement it
tps.reason: Pattern 2 comment said it checks plausibility but just blindly divided by 10. Added pct_limit guard using MINOR_CANN_MAX_FLOWER dict to ensure the correction only fires when the corrected value would be plausible.

tps.title: Pattern 3 blocked by elif chain — never fires after Pattern 1/2
tps.reason: Structural bug. Pattern 3 (minor cannabinoid ceiling) was in an elif after Pattern 1 and 2, so it could never trigger for compounds that didn't match those patterns. Restructured to independent if blocks with corrected_this flag.

tps.title: import copy is local instead of module-level
tps.reason: Minor style issue but valid per PEP 8. Moved import copy to module level with other imports.

fps.title: memory_category vs type overlap
fps.reason: Orthogonal: type=observation/preference, memory_category=durability class

fps.title: %-I platform-specific strftime
fps.reason: Runs only in Docker on Linux where %-I is supported

fps.title: TimeoutError vs asyncio.TimeoutError
fps.reason: In Python 3.11+, TimeoutError IS asyncio.TimeoutError — same class

fps.title: The shutdown path uses two different synchronization patterns for the same asyncio state
fps.reason: Signal handler runs on main thread which is the event loop thread; same-thread asyncio.Event.set() is safe

fps.title: The assembler accepts timing configuration without validation
fps.reason: Config comes from own YAML file, not user input

fps.title: The URL builder interpolates device_id directly into the request path without URL-encoding
fps.reason: device_id values come from internal config (camera names like living_room), not user input

fps.title: audio_url() assembles the query string manually
fps.reason: Query params are integers from internal computation, no encoding risk

fps.title: get_audio() accepts end <= start
fps.reason: Caller always passes clip_start/clip_end from assembler which enforces ordering

fps.title: The time inputs are only partially validated
fps.reason: Inputs are computed internally from epoch timestamps, not user-facing

fps.title: The logger writes the full request URL
fps.reason: URL contains camera name and timestamps, not sensitive data

fps.title: formats timestamps with dt.strftime(%-I:%M %p) which is not portable
fps.reason: Runs in Docker on Linux only, Windows portability not relevant

fps.title: Undefined background task function `_auto_analyze_image` causes runtime failure
fps.reason: Function is defined at line 333 in the full file. Quorum only reviewed partial code submission.

fps.title: Missing import for `FileResponse` breaks blob retrieval endpoint
fps.reason: FileResponse is imported on line 10 of the full file via `from fastapi.responses import FileResponse`. Quorum only reviewed partial code.

fps.title: Mutable list used as class-level default for CORS origins
fps.reason: Pydantic BaseModel/BaseSettings copies defaults per instance — mutable default sharing is not an issue here unlike plain Python classes.

fps.title: Calling `start()` multiple times spawns duplicate stream tasks
fps.reason: The StreamManager._run() method is only called once from create_app's startup handler. There's no code path that calls start() multiple times.

fps.title: Discovery silently ignores `ScryptedUrlResolver` despite accepting it in the type signature
fps.reason: ScryptedApiResolver is the preferred resolver and is used first. ScryptedUrlResolver (go2rtc-based) is used as a fallback in the stream loop, not in _attempt_discovery. The type signature correctly reflects both pathways.

fps.title: Credential-redaction regex is malformed and can leak RTSP credentials to logs
fps.reason: Regex r"://([^:]+):([^@]+)@" with replacement r"://\1:***@" correctly masks passwords. \1 captures username, *** replaces the password between : and @. This is correct behavior.

fps.title: Credential redaction regex does not actually mask passwords
fps.reason: Same regex pattern as stream_manager. r"://([^:]+):([^@]+)@" replaced with r"://\1:***@" correctly masks password with ***. \1 is username, not password.

fps.title: Same broken redaction logic leaks credentials for single_url streams
fps.reason: Same regex, same correct behavior. Masks password with *** while preserving username.

fps.title: Recovery path marks camera online before reconnection succeeds
fps.reason: This fires after a stream that lasted >= STABLE_STREAM_SECONDS and was previously stuck. It's recovering FROM stuck state and re-publishing online status, which is correct — the stream was just alive for 30+ seconds.

fps.title: Result source is hardcoded to PostgreSQL even though the service claims SQLite fallback
fps.reason: SQLite is only for unit tests. Production always uses Postgres. source=postgres is correct.

fps.title: Injected dependency is typed as required but defaults to None
fps.reason: Standard FastAPI Depends() pattern. The default None is overridden by the dependency injection.

fps.title: total reports only the over-fetched subset, not the real match count
fps.reason: total=len(scored) already reports full match count. results=scored[:limit] returns truncated set. Test confirms this is correct behavior.

fps.title: Search query length is unbounded before hitting repository search
fps.reason: Already fixed in router - max_length=500 on the query parameter. Service is only called via router.

fps.title: Unvalidated limit allows negative or extremely large values
fps.reason: Router constrains limit to ge=1, le=100. Service is only called via router.

fps.title: Returned total is inaccurate because results are over-fetched and truncated
fps.reason: Already verified with test - total=len(scored) is the full post-filter count, results=scored[:limit] is the truncated set. Test test_search_total_matches_result_count confirms this. Duplicate finding from prior review.

fps.title: Dependency-injected search service is incorrectly declared with a None default
fps.reason: Standard FastAPI Depends() pattern. Duplicate from prior review.

fps.title: Search query length is unbounded, allowing expensive repository searches
fps.reason: Already capped at max_length=500 in router. Service only called via router. Third time this has been flagged.

fps.title: total undercounts matches because it reports only the over-fetched working set
fps.reason: Already verified with test. total=len(scored) is post-filter count. Fourth time flagged.

fps.title: Search query length is unbounded, enabling expensive backend searches
fps.reason: Router caps at max_length=500. Fifth time flagged. Please stop.

fps.title: User-controlled limit is not validated before being multiplied and passed to the backend
fps.reason: Router validates limit ge=1 le=100. Max overfetch is 300 rows.

fps.title: Filter parameters are unbounded in size
fps.reason: Single-user VPN-only system. content_type and source are short enum-like strings. Already addressed as partial in prior review.

fps.title: Vector search results ignore caller-supplied filters
fps.reason: Pre-existing pattern — text vector search also doesn't apply filters in SQL. Filters are applied post-hoc in the search() method. Image vector search follows the same pattern.

fps.title: Function search has cyclomatic complexity 13
fps.reason: Pre-existing. Adding the image vector block increased it by ~2. The function is linear and readable despite the complexity score.

fps.title: Function patch_image_embedding has cyclomatic complexity 5
fps.reason: Complexity 5 is low and the function is straightforward — validate dims, resolve capture, update DB.

fps.title: Function optimize_prompts_by_format has cyclomatic complexity 12
fps.reason: Moderate complexity is acceptable for optimization logic

fps.title: Function extraction_quality_metric has cyclomatic complexity 5
fps.reason: Complexity 5 is not actionable

fps.title: Function build_optimized_prompt has cyclomatic complexity 6
fps.reason: Low complexity, not actionable

fps.title: Function optimize_extraction_prompt has cyclomatic complexity 5
fps.reason: Low complexity, not actionable

fps.title: Function get_extraction_rate has cyclomatic complexity 6
fps.reason: Low complexity, not actionable

fps.title: Function handle_data_url has cyclomatic complexity 6
fps.reason: Low complexity, not actionable

fps.title: Function validate_api_key has cyclomatic complexity 7
fps.reason: Moderate complexity is acceptable for validation

fps.title: Function validate_totals_consistency has cyclomatic complexity 12
fps.reason: Moderate complexity is acceptable for validation logic

fps.title: Function validate_file has cyclomatic complexity 6
fps.reason: CC=6 barely over threshold of 5. Function is 30 lines and perfectly readable. Noise for an 84-line utility script.

fps.title: Function validate_all has cyclomatic complexity 7
fps.reason: It's a print-report function. CC thresholds are meaningless for straightforward output formatting code.

fps.title: open() without explicit encoding parameter
fps.reason: Duplicate of the I/O error finding which already covers encoding. Shouldn't be a separate finding.

fps.title: Recording updates are never committed to the database
fps.reason: The get_db() dependency auto-commits after yield (database.py L27-35). All mutations within the request are committed when the dependency context exits.

fps.title: Submitted boundary corrections are not persisted
fps.reason: Same as update_recording — the get_db() dependency auto-commits after yield. The correction IS persisted.

fps.title: Declared optimistic locking version is not actually enforced
fps.reason: Optimistic locking IS enforced manually in submit_correction (main.py L307-312) which checks entry.version against the submitted version and returns 409 on mismatch. Not using SQLAlchemy's version_id_col doesn't mean it's unenforced.

fps.title: Directly invoking a Celery task from another task bypasses Celery execution features
fps.reason: Intentional design — comment on L187 explains: "Run sequentially (intentional — SQLite doesn't support concurrent writes)". Direct call is correct here.

fps.title: Whole segments are buffered in memory instead of streamed
fps.reason: This is inherent to using pipe:1 with ffmpeg — the segment must be fully generated before it can be cached or returned. No practical streaming alternative for HLS segments that also need caching.

fps.title: Broad exception handling hides database/query failures
fps.reason: By design — graceful degradation is the core pattern. BM25 falls back to tsvector, tsvector falls back to ILIKE, vector search falls back to empty. Errors are logged as warnings with structlog. Narrowing exceptions would break the fallback chain.

fps.title: Search query and limit are not bounded before hitting FTS/vector backends
fps.reason: Pre-existing. The router layer (search.py) already caps limit to 100 via query parameter validation. The service receives pre-validated inputs.

fps.title: Date filters incorrectly include records with NULL occurred_at
fps.reason: By design. Captures without occurred_at (e.g., imported documents with unknown dates) should not be excluded when the user filters by date range. Excluding them would hide relevant clinical content that lacks a precise timestamp.

fps.title: Function `extract_product_detail` has cyclomatic complexity 5
fps.reason: CC=5 is at threshold boundary and the function is a straightforward sequential extraction flow. Not worth refactoring.

fps.title: Credential-redaction regex is malformed and can leak secrets into logs/events
fps.reason: Hallucinated description. The LLM claimed the pattern was `[^:[REDACTED]@]+` but the actual code uses two separate correct character classes: `r"://([^:]+):([^@]+)@"`. The `***@` in the replacement string confused the model into thinking it was part of the match pattern. Regex is correct and redaction works.

fps.title: Credential redaction regex is malformed and can leak RTSP credentials into logs/events
fps.reason: Same hallucination as the stream_manager finding. Actual code uses `r"://([^:]+):([^@]+)@"` which correctly captures username and password groups separately. The `***@` in the replacement string is confusing the LLM. No leak.

fps.title: Stuck-mode fallback can bypass the resolver/proxy and connect directly to the camera
fps.reason: The `self._camera.rtsp_url` IS the user-configured URL from config.yaml, which for Scrypted-backed cameras is the rebroadcast URL, not a direct camera URL. go2rtc was removed in v0.8.6; there's no separate proxy layer. Reverting to configured URL in stuck mode is intentional fallback behavior — it doesn't bypass anything the user didn't configure. No security/architecture regression.
